summary:
The most clinically significant cytokine linked to periapical lesion pathology is interleukin-1 (IL-1), which drives chronic inflammation through potent pro-inflammatory signaling pathways commonly observed in these infections. This makes it central to understanding both disease progression and potential therapeutic interventions targeting local immune responses.

The primary cytokine implicated in periapical lesion pathogenesis is interleukin-1 (IL-1), as it plays a well-documented role in driving localized chronic inflammation characteristic of infected dental pulp tissue. Its involvement supports targeted anti-inflammatory strategies aimed at modulating host response during infection or healing phases.
